2026-05-28.content:
To enhance security and address potential vulnerabilities (such as GHSA-3m6q-h5gj-7mrw), the Secure Source Manager Git-over-SSH server configuration has removed support for several legacy and insecure SSH algorithms.

SSH clients must support one or more of the following modern algorithms to connect:

* **Key Exchange Algorithms:** `curve25519-sha256`, `diffie-hellman-group14-sha256`
* **Ciphers:** `chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com`, `aes128-ctr`, `aes192-ctr,aes256-ctr`, `aes128-gcm@openssh.com`, `aes256-gcm@openssh.com`
* **MACs:** `hmac-sha2-256-etm@openssh.com`, `hmac-sha2-256`

Users with old or non-standard SSH clients lacking support for these algorithms will be unable to connect using SSH for Git operations. Ensure your SSH client is up-to-date.

summary.title: SSH algorithm update for Secure Source Manager
summary.summary: Secure Source Manager is updating its SSH server configuration to remove support for legacy and insecure algorithms, enhancing security. Users must ensure their SSH clients support modern algorithms like curve25519-sha256 and aes256-ctr to avoid connection issues.

2026-05-27.content:
Secure Source Manager enforces a daily rate quota on the size of code
scanned for credentials per instance. The default quota limit is 1 GB per day
per instance. For more information, see [Quotas and
limits](https://docs.cloud.google.com/secure-source-manager/docs/quotas).

summary.title: New scanning quota for Secure Source Manager
summary.summary: Secure Source Manager now enforces a daily quota for code scanned for credentials. The default limit is 1 GB per day per instance, helping to manage resource usage and costs.

2026-05-06.content: You can now use [CODEOWNERS files](https://docs.cloud.google.com/secure-source-manager/docs/codeowners) to define required reviewers for pull requests.

summary.title: Define required reviewers with CODEOWNERS
summary.summary: You can now use CODEOWNERS files to specify mandatory reviewers for your pull requests. This helps ensure that specific individuals or teams review critical code changes before they are merged, enhancing code quality and security.

2026-02-06.content: You can now [connect to Secure Source Manager](https://docs.cloud.google.com/developer-connect/docs/connect-secure-source-manager) using Developer Connect.

summary.title: Connect to Secure Source Manager
summary.summary: Developer Connect now supports connections to Secure Source Manager. This integration allows you to seamlessly connect your development environment to your private code repositories.

2026-02-03.content:
You can now assign custom domains for your private Secure Source Manager
instance's HTML, API, Git HTTP, and Git SSH endpoints. To use custom domains, you
must provide your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate and CA pool during
instance creation. You can only create custom domains using the API when you
first create an instance. For more information, see [Create a Private Service
Connect Secure Source Manager
instance](https://docs.cloud.google.com/secure-source-manager/docs/create-private-service-connect-instance).

summary.title: Custom domains for Secure Source Manager
summary.summary: You can now assign custom domains for your private Secure Source Manager instance's endpoints. This requires providing your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate and CA pool during instance creation. Custom domains can only be configured using the API when you first create an instance.

2026-02-02.content:
Secure Source Manager is now available in the following [regions](https://docs.cloud.google.com/secure-source-manager/docs/locations#regions):

* us-east1 (South Carolina)

summary.title: Secure Source Manager Now Available
summary.summary: Secure Source Manager is now available in us-east1 (South Carolina). This feature provides a secure and managed environment for your source code repositories. Check the documentation for a full list of supported regions.

2025-09-10.content: Secure Source Manager is [compliant with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act](https://cloud.google.com/security/compliance/hipaa) (HIPAA), and is also [FedRAMP High compliant](https://cloud.google.com/security/compliance/fedramp).

summary.title: Secure Source Manager HIPAA Compliant
summary.summary: Secure Source Manager is compliant with HIPAA.

2025-08-21.content: You can now use a Google-managed certificate when you create a [create a Private Service Connect Secure Source Manager instance](https://cloud.google.com/secure-source-manager/docs/create-private-service-connect-instance#create-private-service-connect-instance).

summary.title: New feature: Google-managed certificates for Private Service Connect
summary.summary: Use a Google-managed certificate for Private Service Connect Secure Source Manager instances.

2025-08-14.content: Integrated data loss prevention (DLP) is now [generally available](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages)

summary.title: Data Loss Prevention Now Available
summary.summary: Integrated data loss prevention is now generally available.

2025-08-04.content: Secure Source Manager is now [generally available](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages) for all customers.

summary.title: Secure Source Manager now available
summary.summary: Secure Source Manager is now available for all customers.

2025-07-08.content:
Secure Source Manager is now available in the following [regions](https://cloud.google.com/secure-source-manager/docs/locations#regions):

* australia-southeast1 (Sydney)
* asia-northeast1 (Tokyo)

summary.title: Secure Source Manager now in more available
summary.summary: Secure Source Manager is now available in australia-southeast1 (Sydney) and asia-northeast1 (Tokyo).
